channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Siouxsie Sioux Became a Defining Figure of Goth Music and Fashion in the 1980s
item.description: The article looks back at the influence and evolution of Siouxsie Sioux, lead singer of Siouxsie and the Banshees, during the 1980s. Born Susan Ballion in 1957, Siouxsie first emerged from the British...

item.title: Matatu Workers Union Warns of Nationwide Strike Over Low Wages and Fuel Crisis
item.description: Matatu workers in Nairobi and other urban centres have threatened to stage industrial action over poor pay and the rising cost of fuel. The warning was issued by the Matatu Workers Union (MWU) togethe...

item.title: Questions raised about ground-penetrating radar findings at Kamloops residential school site and possible septic system interference
item.description: This opinion article examines the controversy surrounding the 2021 announcement by Tk’emlúps te Secwépemc that ground-penetrating radar (GPR) had identified 215 potential burial sites at the former K...

item.title: James Talarico Criticizes Ken Paxton Over Plea Deal in Child Abuse Case After Campaign Insults
item.description: Texas Democratic state representative James Talarico responded sharply to recent insults from Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton following Paxton’s victory over Senator John Cornyn in the Republican Se...

item.title: Trump Says Midterm Elections Do Not Concern Him During Cabinet Meeting, Prompting Online Criticism
item.description: President Donald Trump sparked criticism online after stating during a White House cabinet meeting that he was unconcerned about the upcoming 2026 midterm elections. His comments came while discussing...

item.title: Residents renew noise complaints as Rogers Stadium launches summer concert season with Bruno Mars
item.description: Residents living near Rogers Stadium in Toronto are once again raising concerns about excessive noise as the venue begins its summer concert season with performances by Bruno Mars. According to the re...

item.title: Police identify 58-year-old victim in Dunedin homicide investigation
item.description: Authorities in Dunedin, New Zealand, have formally identified the man found dead at a residential property on Somerville Street in Andersons Bay as Douglas Alexander Senior, aged 58. The discovery was...

item.title: Trump’s primary interventions fuel Republican backlash and intra-party tensions, analysts say
item.description: A political analysis published in The Washington Post and cited by Raw Story argues that President Donald Trump’s increasing involvement in Republican primary battles is creating growing tensions with...

item.title: Ruiru Boda Boda Rider Injured by Rubber Bullets During Matatu Strike Protests
item.description: A boda boda rider identified as Daniel Gichimu is recovering after he was allegedly shot seven times with rubber bullets during demonstrations linked to a matatu operators’ strike in Ruiru, Kiambu Cou...

item.title: Jared Polis Faces Criticism After Clemency Decision and Controversial Party Call Appearance
item.description: Colorado Governor Jared Polis faced significant backlash following his decision to grant clemency to former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, who had been convicted in 2024 for allowing unauthorized acce...

item.title: Seasonal Wildflower Bloom Transforms Namaqualand Desert After Rainfall
item.description: Namaqualand, located in the northwestern region of South Africa, is one of the driest landscapes in southern Africa for most of the year. However, following seasonal winter rains, the region undergoes...

item.title: MS NOW Stops Live Coverage to Fact-Check Trump's Claims About Prescription Drug Prices
item.description: During a televised Cabinet meeting, President Donald Trump made a series of claims about prescription drug pricing and the success of his administration’s drug discount initiatives. Trump stated that ...

item.title: FBI morale reportedly shaken after senior official firing sparks internal concern
item.description: A report from MS NOW, as cited in Raw Story, describes growing unease within the FBI following the abrupt firing of Deputy Assistant Director Emily Morales. According to journalist Ken Dilanian, Moral...

item.title: CDF Asim Munir visits frontline troops in Zhob on Eid, vows continued action against terrorism
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir spent Eidul Azha with frontline troops stationed in Zhob, Balochistan, according to a statement issued by the Inter-Service...

item.title: Physician questions missing clinical details in Trump’s health reports amid concerns over swelling and fatigue
item.description: A recent analysis of President Donald Trump’s reported health status has raised questions among medical observers after his third hospital visit in 13 months. The visit, described by the White House a...

item.title: Food prices projected to rise due to climate and supply pressures ahead of elections
item.description: The article reports that American consumers may face a new surge in grocery prices driven by a combination of extreme weather, geopolitical tensions, and ongoing trade policy effects. After already ex...

item.title: New Zealand to Introduce AI-Assisted Breast Cancer Screening in 2027
item.description: The New Zealand government has announced plans to introduce artificial intelligence technology into the national breast cancer screening program beginning in early 2027. The AI system will serve as an...

item.title: Scientists Officially Identify New Deep-Sea Octopus Species Near the Galápagos Islands
item.description: Researchers coordinated by Chicago's Field Museum have officially identified and named a new species of deep-sea octopus discovered near the Galápagos Islands. The species, named Microeledone galapage...

item.title: Economic Hardship Makes Ilorin Muslims Buy Eid Rams on Credit with Interest
item.description: Na real matter wey dey happen for Ilorin as economic hardship under Tinubu government don push many Muslim faithful to buy rams for Eid-el-Kabir on credit with added interest. Prices of sacrificial an...

item.title: Kennedy Center executive warns court that removing Trump’s name could threaten fundraising and financial stability
item.description: A senior official at the Kennedy Center has told a federal judge that removing President Donald Trump’s name from the performing arts venue could severely damage its fundraising ability and long-term ...
